discovery.run_summary: All six candidates ship with earnings 8-K cover documents only (fell_back_to_primary), so the prepared-financial detail in Exhibit 99.1 is not visible. Reads must rely on 10-K business descriptions and 10-Q Risk Factors deltas. Two situations stand out with filing-grounded signals: MOD (Reverse Morris Trust spin/merger with Gentherm pending, fresh transaction risk factors in 10-Q) and ASAN (recent CEO/COO/General Counsel turnover and AI execution narrative). MDB shows a recent CEO change and a Q3 pre-announcement 8-K. Others (BAH, BOX, ESTC) lack actionable directional asymmetry from filings alone.

discoveries.name: Modine Manufacturing
discoveries.filings_evidence: MOD's 10-Q Risk Factors (filing_date 2026-02-05) add multiple fresh, situation-specific risk factors disclosing a pending Reverse Morris Trust transaction spinning Performance Technologies and merging it with Gentherm — explicitly flagging employee distraction, customer/supplier deferral of decisions, significant transaction costs incurred whether or not it closes, potential litigation, and the warning that the remaining MOD will be 'less diversified, with a more concentrated focus on the data center and commercial HVAC and refrigeration markets.' The 10-K business section also flagged that the Performance Technologies segment commercial vehicle, agriculture, and off-highway markets were already weak going into FY26.
discoveries.guidance_pattern: Cannot assess from cover-only 8-Ks; press release exhibits not parsed. Modine has historically issued full-year guidance with quarterly updates around its fiscal Q-ends.
discoveries.setup: Q4 FY26 print four trading days out, with a major announced spin/merger transaction and pre-existing softness in agriculture/off-highway/commercial vehicle markets in the segment being separated.
discoveries.thesis: The pending Gentherm RMT transaction introduces specific, filings-acknowledged execution and distraction risks (employee, customer, supplier) in the same Performance Technologies segment whose end markets the 10-K already described as deteriorating. Management commentary around a quarter this close to a complex spin tends to surface caution, transaction costs, and conservative segment outlooks — risks the market may be underweighting if focused on the data center growth story.
discoveries.what_confirms: Soft Performance Technologies revenue and margin in the print; explicit mention of transaction-related expenses; cautious FY27 framing tied to spin uncertainty; any customer/order deferral language consistent with the new 10-Q risk factor.
discoveries.what_kills: Data Center Cooling outperformance (it was 25% of FY25 revenue and growing fast) large enough to dominate the narrative; clean reaffirmation of transaction timeline with no negative segment surprises; PT segment stabilizing rather than deteriorating.
discoveries.what_to_learn: When a company adds multi-paragraph, situation-specific Risk Factors in a 10-Q about a pending corporate separation, the next print often carries transaction-cost noise and softer near-term commentary in the segment being separated — even if the remaining business is strong.
research_pointers: Check Performance Technologies organic revenue trajectory and margin vs. prior quarters
Look for transaction-related expense line items and any update on RMT timing/IRS ruling
Compare Data Center Cooling growth rate vs prior quarter (was the dominant Climate Solutions driver)
Listen for customer or order deferral commentary in PT segment

discoveries.name: Asana
discoveries.filings_evidence: 10-K (filing_date 2026-03-13) discloses dollar-based net retention of only 97% for Core customers and 96% for $100K+ customers as of Jan 31, 2026 — both below 100%, indicating contraction within the existing base. The 10-K also flags a June 2025 security incident with the Model Context Protocol feature that 'potentially led to certain data in the instances of certain customers being accessible to other users.' The Dec 2025 8-K shows simultaneous COO and General Counsel resignations effective Dec 31, 2025. Q3 FY26 press release (Dec 2, 2025) refers to 'updated guidance for the fourth quarter and full fiscal year 2026.' Net loss was still $189M for FY26.
discoveries.guidance_pattern: Cover-only 8-Ks indicate management has issued quarterly guidance updates; cannot verify beat/miss cadence from these filings alone, but the Q3 release explicitly updated FY guidance.
discoveries.setup: Q1 FY27 print with NRR sub-100% on the books, recent senior executive turnover (COO, GC, both effective Dec 31, 2025), and an AI-product pivot ('Agentic Enterprise') whose monetization is unproven in the filings.
discoveries.thesis: Filings show retention deterioration (NRR below 100% on the highest-spend cohorts) coexisting with a heavy narrative pivot to AI/Agentic Enterprise that is not yet quantified as revenue contribution. Combined with executive departures and a disclosed security flaw in an AI-adjacent feature, the next print is more likely to surface continued NRR pressure and elevated AI investment than to deliver an upside catalyst — particularly if guidance was raised in Q3 on AI optimism.
discoveries.what_confirms: NRR flat or declining further in Q1 FY27; cautious FY27 guide; rising opex tied to AI investment without commensurate AI Studio/AI Teammates revenue disclosure; commentary about extended sales cycles consistent with the 10-Q risk language.
discoveries.what_kills: NRR rebounds above 100%; a specific, quantified AI Studio/AI Teammates revenue contribution disclosure; clean reaffirmation of FY27 with strong large-customer growth (817 over-100K customers expanding).
discoveries.what_to_learn: When a SaaS company's NRR is already below 100% in two cohorts and senior leaders depart in the same quarter as a strategic AI pivot, the next print's risk skews to disappointment unless the company can quantify the new product's revenue.
research_pointers: Track NRR for Core and $100K+ cohorts — did either turn back up?
Quantify AI Studio + AI Teammates contribution to ARR if disclosed
Watch for any further executive departures or replacement announcements
Look for color on enterprise sales cycle length given macro language in 10-Q risk factors
Check whether stock repurchase authorization increase (Feb 2026) signals management view on share price

skipped.reason: All four earnings 8-Ks are cover documents only (fell_back_to_primary); cannot read prepared financials, guidance pattern, or book-to-bill. 10-K and 10-Q risk factors disclose ongoing federal contract reviews/cancellations under new administration but with no quantification — direction unclear from filings alone. UNCLEAR.

All earnings 8-Ks are cover documents only. 10-K shows non-cancellable backlog growth ($1.06B vs $861M) and stable 104% NRR, but with mixed signals (NRR only modestly up, 'customers' lower headcount growth and greater budget scrutiny' called out). No clean directional edge from filings — RATIONAL.

All earnings 8-Ks are cover documents only. 10-K shows Elastic Cloud growing to 46% of revenue and customer concentration (one channel partner 12% of revenue), but no Q-over-Q financial trajectory visible without press release content. Filings consistent with expectations — RATIONAL/UNCLEAR.

All earnings 8-Ks are cover documents only, though the Nov 3, 2025 8-K contains a pre-announcement of preliminary Q3 results bundled with a CEO change (Dev Ittycheria out, CJ Desai in). The 10-Q discloses 'slower than historical growth of our existing Atlas applications' due to macro, but with new CEO arriving Nov 9, 2025, the May 2026 print is the first full Desai-quarter and direction is genuinely two-sided (kitchen-sink risk vs reset-the-bar opportunity). UNCLEAR.

discovery.no_signals_note: Cover-only 8-Ks across the entire slate substantially impaired the read on guidance cadence and financial trajectory; the two flags rely on 10-K/10-Q text evidence (NRR disclosed in MDA-adjacent business section for ASAN; transaction risk factor additions for MOD) rather than on press release content.
